reforms.description: The Czech Government is planning to set up an innovation fund with the participation of the European Investment Fund to address the low levels of venture capital available for seed and start-up enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of an innovation fund aims to provide necessary financial resources for early-stage innovative companies, thereby fostering entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is planning to set up an innovation fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of an innovation fund represents a significant structural change aimed at addressing the financing gap for innovative start-ups, which is crucial for enhancing the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The government is planning to set up an innovation fund with the participation of the European Investment Fund.

reforms.description: The Czech Government aims to increase and better target R&D and innovation funding to enhance the economy's position in the global value chain, with a focus on encouraging higher business R&D spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing and better targeting of R&D funding is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity, thereby enhancing economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in RDI spending should be continued as it is a key element for upgrading the economy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is essential for improving the innovation landscape and economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The increase in RDI spending should be continued as it is a key element for upgrading the economy in the global value chain.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic plans to accelerate the creation of funds and guarantee programs to support SMEs and innovation, addressing the difficulties in accessing finance for startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to finance for startups, the initiative is expected to foster innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to accelerate the creation of funds and guarantee programs was made in the OECD Economic Survey.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses a critical barrier to innovation and entrepreneurship in the Czech Republic.
reforms.source_quote: Accelerate the creation of funds and guarantee programs to support SMEs and innovation.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic is encouraged to better target government R&D support and streamline innovation policies to enhance productivity growth, particularly by increasing knowledge-based capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the targeting of R&D support is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in firms, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Policies are needed to foster domestic sources of productivity growth.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how R&D support is allocated, aiming to address structural productivity issues in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Better targeting of government R&D support and more focused innovation policies that would be aided by a streamlining of policy institutions and interventions are necessary.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic has increased its R&D spending from 1.1% of GDP in 2003 to 2% of GDP in 2014, aiming to enhance its innovation system and support economic upgrading in global value chains.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: R&D spending has steadily increased over the last decade.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the national innovation system, which is crucial for economic competitiveness and productivity growth.
reforms.source_quote: In the last ten years Czech spending on R&D has increased steadily from 1.1% of GDP in 2003 to 2% of GDP in 2014.

reforms.description: The Czech government is planning to introduce a global evaluation framework for R&D support to improve the assessment of research outputs and benefits, addressing current criticisms of the existing evaluation system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A robust evaluation framework is expected to enhance the effectiveness of R&D funding, ensuring resources are allocated to high-potential projects.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is planning to introduce a global evaluation framework.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to improve the allocation of R&D funding, which is crucial for enhancing innovation outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The government is planning to introduce a global evaluation framework, including improvements in the information system, to assess research outputs and more general benefits.

reforms.description: The Technology Agency of the Czech Republic (TACR) provides targeted support for applied research and experimental development, contributing to increased competitiveness and economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By funding applied research, TACR enhances the innovation capacity of firms, leading to productivity improvements and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: TACR was established in 2009 and has been operational since.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of TACR represents a significant structural change in the support for applied research and innovation in the Czech Republic.
reforms.source_quote: The Technology Agency of the Czech Republic (TACR) is part of the state system of support for applied RDI.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase access to capital for SMEs and facilitate the implementation of innovative projects through the National Innovation Fund, aimed at addressing financing gaps for startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to startups, the initiative aims to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, which can enhance economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is planning to increase access to capital from public funds for SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a critical gap in financing for startups, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: To remedy these financing gaps for SMEs and start-ups, the government is planning to increase access to capital from public funds.

reforms.description: The government aims to streamline the administration and implementation of innovation policy to reduce fragmentation and improve efficiency in R&D support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the efficiency of innovation policy administration, the reform is expected to enhance the effectiveness of R&D funding and support.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy design and implementation of innovation policies should be streamlined.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses systemic inefficiencies in the innovation support framework, which can lead to better outcomes for R&D investments.
reforms.source_quote: The organisation and administration of R&D and innovation policies is still too complex.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic aims to increase the share of government R&D grants directed towards international collaborations, enhancing technology and knowledge transfer under Czech leadership.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased international collaboration can enhance the quality and applicability of research, leading to greater innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: A greater part of government R&D grants should be directed to programmes including international collaborations under Czech leadership.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy, focusing on international collaboration which is crucial for enhancing the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: A greater part of government R&D grants should be directed to programmes including international collaborations under Czech leadership.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic is examining the simplification of the registration process for start-up companies to promote easier access to business establishment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying the registration process can lower barriers to entry for new businesses, fostering entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The one-stop-shop for start-up companies... is still under examination by the parliament.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses a key barrier to entrepreneurship, potentially leading to increased start-up activity and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The one-stop-shop for start-up companies... is still under examination by the parliament.

reforms.description: The Czech government has established the Czech-Moravian Guarantee and Development Bank (CMZRB) to support small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) through various financial instruments, including guarantees and soft loans.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to finance for SMEs, the initiative aims to enhance the growth of innovative firms, thereby boosting productivity and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The bank has expanded its activities to support SMEs and provide various financial services.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of CMZRB represents a significant structural change in the support framework for SMEs, which are crucial for innovation and economic growth in the Czech Republic.
reforms.source_quote: The Czech-Moravian Guarantee and Development Bank, Inc. (CMZRB) was founded in 1992 as a specialised banking institution to support small and medium-sized enterprises.

reforms.description: The INOSTART programme is designed to support innovative start-ups by providing guarantees for loans up to 60% of the outstanding loan principal, allowing novice entrepreneurs to access credit more easily.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to credit for innovative start-ups, the programme encourages entrepreneurship and the development of new businesses, which can enhance economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been piloted and later extended to the entire country.
reforms.importance_rationale: The INOSTART programme represents a significant effort to support innovative start-ups, which are crucial for economic dynamism and job creation.
reforms.source_quote: The INOSTART programme is focused on supporting innovative start-ups characterised by larger risks, a short financial history and low collateral to raise funds.

reforms.description: The CMZRB provides guarantees for loans to SMEs, facilitating access to bank financing and supporting social entrepreneurs who employ disadvantaged people.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to financing for SMEs, the programme enhances their ability to invest and grow, contributing to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been operational and provides guarantees for loans.
reforms.importance_rationale: The CMZRB's guarantee programmes are significant for enhancing the financial viability of SMEs, which are vital for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The guarantee programme aims to facilitate access to bank financing for SMEs.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic aims to develop government co-financing schemes to complement grants and increase fiscal incentives for business R&D spending, thereby enhancing the overall effectiveness of R&D funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing fiscal incentives for R&D, the reform is expected to stimulate private sector investment in research, leading to higher productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to develop co-financing schemes has been made as part of the strategy to enhance R&D funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy aimed at improving the effectiveness of R&D investments, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: Develop government co-financing schemes to complement grants and increase fiscal incentives for business R&D spending.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic plans to unify the design, assessment, and coordination of research and development and innovation policies under a single institution to streamline efforts and improve policy effectiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By consolidating R&D policy efforts, the reform is expected to enhance coherence and efficiency in innovation governance, leading to better outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to unify R&D policies has been made to improve coordination and effectiveness.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to create a more integrated approach to R&D policy, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Unify the design, assessment and coordination of research and development and innovation policies in a single institution.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic intends to increase R&D spending effectiveness by better targeting government funding to broaden the scope of R&D activities, enhancing the overall impact of public investments in research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the targeting of R&D funding is expected to lead to more effective use of resources, fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to increase R&D spending effectiveness has been made to broaden the scope of activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the impact of public R&D investments, which is crucial for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Increase R&D spending effectiveness by better targeting government funding to broaden the scope of R&D activities.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic plans to increase incentives and funding through national programmes of applied research and innovations to foster collaboration between research entities and businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between research and business is expected to lead to more innovative solutions and improved productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to increase incentives and funding for collaboration has been made to enhance applied research.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to strengthen the link between research and industry, which is essential for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Increase incentives and funding through the national programmes of applied research and innovations to develop collaboration between research entities and businesses.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic intends to develop mobility schemes for public researchers to work in businesses’ research centres, facilitating interactions between research institutions and firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating mobility between public research and private sector research is expected to enhance knowledge transfer and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to develop mobility schemes has been made to facilitate interactions between research institutions and firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the collaboration between public and private research, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Develop mobility schemes for public researchers to work during some period in businesses’ research centres to facilitate the interactions between research institutions and firms.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic plans to use public procurement contracts to initiate innovative solutions in strategic areas with societal benefits, thereby leveraging public spending for innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Using public procurement to stimulate innovation is expected to drive the development of new solutions that address societal challenges.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to use public procurement for innovative solutions has been made to leverage public spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to utilize public procurement as a tool for innovation, which can lead to societal benefits.
reforms.source_quote: Use public procurement contracts to initiate innovative solutions in strategic areas with societal benefits.
